tweet: My colleague @NormaJTorres lost her own home to a wildfire in 2005 — she asserts we must prepare our broader community at every level for wildfire response.

name: Rep. Katie Hill
tweet: We cannot be complacent as wildfires continue to devastate our communities. It would be irresponsible to pretend that fire seasons today are no different from fire seasons of the past. Yes, this is our new normal, but we can and must do better.

name: Rep. Katie Hill
tweet: Last month I introduced an amendment on the House floor to address our new wildfire reality. The amendment increases funding for wildfire preparedness, suppression, and emergency rehabilitation bringing funding for these critical issues to a total of $5.2 billion.

name: Rep. Katie Hill
tweet: Today, we also need to talk about the root causes of these wildfires and their impacts on our communities and acknowledge that climate change has been a major driver of the destruction that has been wrought throughout our states.

name: Rep. Katie Hill
tweet: I cannot express how grateful I am for the tireless work of our dedicated and courageous firefighters and first responders. We must all do our part to prevent these wildfires from burning through our neighborhoods and work together to mitigate the damage when they do.

name: Rep. Katie Hill

name: Rep. Katie Hill
tweet: My family and I have been personally impacted by these fires. I had to evacuate from my home last summer — we had to trailer my horse, and relocate my other animals to my sister’s house, only for my sister later be evacuated from her home too. And that wasn’t the first time.

tweet: 3. The #ViolenceAgainstWomenAct closes the "boyfriend loophole" which would stop partners convicted of abuse or stalking from being able to buy a gun. This is especially needed since we know domestic abuse is one of the biggest predictors of mass shootings. Again. No-brainer.

tweet: John Kostelnik is the President for AFGE 3969 Local — representing around a thousand federal correctional workers at Victorville.   “I got staff out there with medical issues — with cancer. And right now their medical bills and insurance aren’t getting paid.”

tweet: “I want to thank the Congresswoman for doing this. This is why she was elected. I don’t want to send people to DC who are just going to occupy time [...] if you go to work — you should get paid.” — George McCubbin, AFGE

name: Rep. Katie Hill
tweet: George McCubbin is the National Vice President of @AFGENational — he drove all the way up from San Diego to be with us today.   He is a retired Border Patrol agent who says that this shutdown is due to a lack of leadership in DC.

name: Rep. Katie Hill
tweet: We’re joined by Palmdale Mayor, Steven Hofbauer, who notes that this fight is not one that the public wants to have — by and large, we agree on issues like border security.   We also agree that this shutdown hurts workers and families in #CA25.

name: Rep. Katie Hill
tweet: Thank you for joining our town hall live-tweet! Katie’s staff, here!  Katie is discussing some of the biggest effects of the shutdown, including:  -Tax return delays -Reduced funding for #SNAP -400,000 federal employees who will miss their paychecks if this continues past 1/11

name: Rep. Katie Hill
tweet: We have to do more. It’s that simple. Americans shouldn’t be shot in bowling allies — or anywhere. My heart is with those whose lives were affected or ended by the shooting in Torrance last night. Thank you to the incredible first responders and law enforcement at the scene.

name: Rep. Katie Hill
tweet: @UMadYet1 @NancyPelosi We’re bringing you H.R.1 — it’ll be the first piece of legislation on the floor of the 116th Congress and it’s full of solutions for the problems we discussed here.

name: Rep. Katie Hill
tweet: Our communities sent me and my fellow Members-elect to Washington with a call to action – get dark money out of politics, clean up corruption, and make sure every vote and every voice is heard. Let's make good on that promise. #ForThePeople  https://t.co/0h5db7QVUx

name: Rep. Katie Hill
tweet: After the devastating fires in California, our communities need relief and they need it now.  It’s unusual for Members-elect to make federal requests before inauguration, but we promised our communities that we would start fighting for them on day one. That’s what we’re doing.  https://t.co/A6fxMwfT8T
